questions.q: In which year was the First Battle of Panipat fought?
questions.explanation: The First Battle of Panipat was fought in 1526 CE between Babur and Ibrahim Lodi.

questions.q: Who led the victorious forces at the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur, a Timurid prince from Central Asia, led the victorious army at Panipat in 1526.

questions.q: Who was the defeated ruler at the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi, the last Sultan of the Lodi dynasty, was defeated and killed at Panipat.

questions.q: Which empire was founded in India as a result of the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur's victory at Panipat established the Mughal Empire in India.

questions.q: Which Indian state did the First Battle of Panipat bring to an end?
questions.explanation: The defeat of Ibrahim Lodi ended the Delhi Sultanate, which had ruled since 1206.

questions.q: The town of Panipat lies in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Panipat is located in present-day Haryana, north of Delhi.

questions.q: Which type of weaponry helped Babur's smaller army defeat the Lodi forces?
questions.explanation: Babur's use of field artillery and matchlock guns was decisive at Panipat in 1526.

questions.q: From which region did Babur, the victor of Panipat, originally come?
questions.explanation: Babur was a Timurid prince from Central Asia, descended from Timur and Genghis Khan.

questions.q: Babur's forces at Panipat were generally described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Babur's army was outnumbered but triumphed through superior tactics and artillery.

questions.q: The First Battle of Panipat marked the beginning of which era in Indian history?
questions.explanation: The battle marked the start of Mughal rule in the Indian subcontinent.

questions.q: The defensive tactic Babur used at Panipat, lashing carts together to shield his guns, is associated with which name?
questions.explanation: Babur arranged carts tied with ropes, a tactic often linked to the Ottoman use of wagons (araba) to protect artillery.

questions.q: Which flanking tactic, dividing the army into wings to encircle the enemy, did Babur employ at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur used the tulughma, a Central Asian tactic of flanking divisions to surround the Lodi army.

questions.q: Which master gunner is credited with commanding Babur's artillery at the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ali Quli is the gunner credited with managing Babur's cannon at Panipat in 1526; the Ottoman gunner Mustafa Rumi is chiefly associated with the later Battle of Khanwa.

questions.q: Babur recorded his account of the Panipat campaign in which famous work?
questions.explanation: Babur described his conquests, including Panipat, in his memoir the Baburnama.

questions.q: Roughly how large was Ibrahim Lodi's army at Panipat according to traditional accounts?
questions.explanation: Traditional accounts put Ibrahim Lodi's force at roughly 100,000 men, far larger than Babur's army.

questions.q: What distinction does Ibrahim Lodi hold regarding his death at Panipat?
options: He was the only Delhi Sultan to die on a battlefield
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi is the only Sultan of Delhi known to have been killed in battle, dying at Panipat.

questions.q: After his victory at Panipat, which city did Babur's forces move on to occupy first?
questions.explanation: Following the battle, Babur secured Delhi, with his son Humayun pressing on toward Agra.

questions.q: Which dynasty did Ibrahim Lodi belong to?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was the last ruler of the Lodi dynasty, the final dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Babur claimed descent from which two famous conquerors?
options: Mahmud of Ghazni and Muhammad of Ghor
questions.explanation: Babur was a Timurid on his father's side and descended from Genghis Khan on his mother's side.

questions.q: Which earlier Babur victory in the Punjab set the stage for his march to Panipat?
options: His capture of Lahore and Dipalpur
questions.explanation: Babur's earlier campaigns in the Punjab, including Lahore and Dipalpur, paved the way for his advance to Panipat.

questions.q: Discontent among which group within Ibrahim Lodi's realm contributed to invitations urging Babur to invade?
options: Afghan nobles and the governor of Punjab
questions.explanation: Disaffected Afghan nobles and Daulat Khan Lodi, governor of Punjab, encouraged Babur's invasion.

questions.q: On approximately which date in April 1526 was the First Battle of Panipat fought?
questions.explanation: The battle was fought on or around 21 April 1526.

questions.q: Babur stated this was which numbered expedition he had launched into Hindustan?
questions.explanation: In the Baburnama, Babur counted the Panipat campaign as his fifth expedition into Hindustan.

questions.q: Which Lodi ally and ruler of Gwalior is traditionally said to have died fighting alongside Ibrahim Lodi at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Vikramaditya (Vikramjit) of Gwalior is traditionally said to have fallen at Panipat fighting for Ibrahim Lodi.

questions.q: Approximately how many days did Babur and Ibrahim Lodi face each other before the decisive engagement at Panipat?
options: About a week of skirmishing and standoff
A single afternoon with no delay
questions.explanation: The two armies faced off for roughly a week, with minor skirmishes, before the main battle was joined.

questions.q: Babur reportedly entrusted the front of his line at Panipat to a defensive screen made largely of what?
options: Hundreds of bullock carts and gun emplacements
A line of trained war elephants
questions.explanation: Babur fortified his center with hundreds of carts tied together, with gaps left for his guns and cavalry.

questions.q: Which son of Babur played a prominent role in the campaign and was sent ahead toward Agra after the victory?
questions.explanation: Humayun, Babur's eldest son, took part in the campaign and advanced on Agra after Panipat.

questions.q: The Koh-i-Noor diamond is traditionally said to have come into Babur's family's possession in connection with which post-Panipat event?
options: A tribute from the Nizam of Hyderabad
The gift from the family of the slain Raja of Gwalior to Humayun at Agra
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the great diamond was given to Humayun by the family of the Gwalior raja at Agra after Panipat.

questions.q: Babur's victory at Panipat left him facing which formidable Rajput coalition the following year?
options: Rana Sanga's Rajput confederacy at Khanwa
questions.explanation: In 1527, the year after Panipat, Babur defeated Rana Sanga's Rajput confederacy at the Battle of Khanwa.

questions.q: How long had the Delhi Sultanate lasted by the time it ended at Panipat in 1526?
questions.explanation: The Delhi Sultanate, founded in 1206, lasted about 320 years before ending at Panipat in 1526.
